title: Project Officer (Fellow Engagement) – Local Pathways Fellowship | Remote Global Opportunity with SDSN Youth
summary: If you’re passionate about sustainable cities, youth empowerment, and global collaboration, this opportunity with UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN) Youth could be a powerful stepping stone in your journey. The Project Officer (Fellow Engagement) role within the Local Pathways Fellowship (LPF) offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of urban innovation, global youth leadership, and sustainable development—all from anywhere in the world. About SDSN and Its Global Mission The UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN), established in 2012 under the leadership of the UN Secretary-General, is a global initiative dedicated to mobilizing scientific and technological expertise to drive sustainable development. Its key objectives include: Supporting the implementation of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Advancing the Paris Climate Agreement Promoting integrated solutions to global economic, environmental, and social challenges Encouraging global collaboration and knowledge-sharing About SDSN Youth Launched in 2015, SDSN Youth is a dynamic global platform designed to empower young people to lead sustainable development initiatives. Its focus areas include: Raising awareness about sustainable development Building youth capacity through training and mentorship Encouraging advocacy and collaboration across borders Supporting global data partnerships for development About the Local Pathways Fellowship (LPF) The Local Pathways Fellowship is a 10-month training and peer-learning program that equips young urban leaders with the tools to create impactful, localized solutions for sustainable cities. Through this fellowship, participants: Learn how to localize SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities) Collaborate with peers and local authorities Design and implement innovative urban projects Contribute to building inclusive, resilient, and sustainable cities Role Overview: Project Officer (Fellow Engagement) As a Project Officer, you will play a central role in enhancing the experience and engagement of fellows across the LPF network. You’ll operate within a global, virtual team and contribute to three core pillars: 1. Content Training Support training initiatives that empower fellows to localize SDGs Help fellows build practical skills in sustainable urban development 2. Visibility Engagement Strengthen a global network of 240+ young urban leaders Promote youth voices in international discussions Enhance the visibility of LPF and its impact 3. Administration Management Ensure smooth program operations Maintain databases and communication channels Support long-term scalability of the fellowship Key Responsibilities Fellow Engagement Collaborate with fellows to strengthen peer-to-peer networks Identify fellows’ needs and improve engagement strategies Develop tools and systems for better collaboration Co-create strategies for a more engaging fellowship experience General Administration Manage LPF operations and communication channels Respond to program-related inquiries Maintain platforms such as: Google Drive Workplace Airtable Record and distribute meeting minutes Keep databases and mailing lists updated Processes Operations Support the application and screening process Assist with onboarding new fellows Optimize workflows and operational systems Qualifications and Requirements Minimum Requirements Candidates must: Have knowledge or interest in: Urban planning Urban sustainability Urban technology or related fields Possess experience in: Operations management or membership coordination Demonstrate: Strong organizational and leadership skills Excellent communication and teamwork abilities Be able to: Work across multiple time zones Communicate proactively and consistently Be aged 18–30 years (exceptions may apply) Preferred Qualifications Experience in sustainable urban development Passion for SDGs and climate action Strong English communication skills Ability to commit time and resources effectively Duration, Commitment, and Conditions Duration: 12 months (including a 3-month trial period) Weekly Commitment: Minimum of 10 hours Work Mode: Fully remote (global participation) Compensation: Voluntary (unpaid) Reporting: Directly to the LPF Project Lead Training: Mandatory onboarding and continuous learning modules Note: There is no specified application deadline , so early application is strongly encouraged. Benefits of Joining the LPF Team This role offers more than just experience—it provides a transformative journey into global development leadership: Global Exposure Work in a multicultural, international environment Collaborate with global youth leaders and experts Skill Development Gain hands-on experience in: Program management Stakeholder engagement Sustainable development practices Career Advancement Represent SDSN Youth at global events Build a personal brand in sustainability and SDGs Expand your professional network across sectors Continuous Learning Access training and capacity-building opportunities Develop both personal and professional competencies Why This Opportunity Matters The world urgently needs innovative, youth-driven solutions to urban challenges. By joining the Local Pathways Fellowship as a Project Officer, you become part of a global movement working toward the 2030 Agenda. CLICK HERE FOR MORE AND TO APPLY You won’t just support a program—you’ll help shape the future of sustainable cities. Click Here For more global fellowship opportunities The post Project Officer (Fellow Engagement) – Local Pathways Fellowship | Remote Global Opportunity with SDSN Youth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Join the Eurochild Children’s Council 2026–2028 | Fully Funded Youth Advocacy Opportunity
summary: Are you passionate about children’s rights and ready to make your voice heard across Europe? The 5th Eurochild Children’s Council (ECC) is now accepting applications for its 2026–2028 cohort. This is a unique opportunity for young people to influence decision-making, advocate for change, and collaborate with peers from across the continent. What is the Eurochild Children’s Council (ECC)? The Eurochild Children’s Council (ECC) is a group of 12 children from different European countries who ensure that children’s voices, perspectives, and lived experiences are central to Eurochild’s work. ECC members: Serve a two-year mandate Are nominated by Eurochild member organisations Represent diverse backgrounds, genders, and regions across Europe Who Coordinates the ECC? The ECC is led by Eurochild’s Child Participation Team, ensuring a safe, inclusive, and meaningful experience for all members: Carine – Child Participation Coordinator Safeguarding Focal Person Laure Pailleau – Child Participation Policy Officer (main contact for meetings and coordination) Child Participation Intern – Supporting ECC activities and logistics Members may also collaborate with other Eurochild teams such as communications and advocacy. How Does the ECC Work? ECC activities are structured to ensure active participation and collaboration: Monthly online meetings (approx. 1.5 hours) Annual in-person meeting (approx. 3 days, fully funded) Communication via Basecamp , a secure platform for discussions, document sharing, and collaboration What Can You Expect as an ECC Member? Joining the ECC is both impactful and exciting. Members get to: Participate in High-Level Activities Speak at events in the European Parliament during World Children’s Day Meet Members of the European Parliament (MEPs) Join and help organise international conferences Lead and Contribute Train adults on child participation Design and run workshops Conduct surveys to gather children’s opinions Create child-friendly versions of EU documents Interview candidates applying to work at Eurochild Build Skills and Gain Experience As an ECC member, you will: Receive training in advocacy, public speaking, communication, and EU systems Participate in decision-making at both organisational and European levels Develop skills in teamwork, leadership, and critical thinking Work on important topics like mental health, governance, and children’s rights Eligibility Criteria: Who Can Apply? Eurochild is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity. Applications are encouraged from children of all backgrounds, especially underrepresented groups. To apply, you must: Be aged 10–17 (born between July 2010 and July 2016) Speak conversational English Be actively involved in a Eurochild member organisation Be available from July 2026 to July 2028 Commit to monthly online meetings and occasional additional tasks Be able to attend at least one in-person meeting per year (costs covered) Be open-minded and willing to collaborate with peers from different cultures Be motivated to speak up on issues affecting children How to Apply for the ECC Step 1: Talk to Your Organisation Confirm that your organisation can support your application throughout the two-year mandate. If multiple children apply, the organisation may conduct a pre-selection. Step 2: Prepare Your Application Complete the application form Answer written questions Record a short video (instructions provided in the form) Step 3: Submit Your Application Deadline: 30 April 2026 APPLY HERE What Happens After You Apply? May 2026: Applications reviewed by Eurochild staff and current ECC members Interviews: May be conducted if needed Results: Announced by 31 May 2026 If selected: Consent forms must be completed by you, your parent/guardian, and your organisation You’ll be invited to a first online onboarding meeting Join an Info Session Interested applicants can attend an online info session to learn more and ask questions: Thursday, 12 March at 17:00 (CET) Wednesday, 18 March at 17:00 (CET) You’ll also meet current ECC members who will share their experiences. Have Questions? For any inquiries during the application process: Email: childparticipation@eurochild.org Be sure to include a parent or guardian in your email The Eurochild Children’s Council is more than just a program—it’s a platform for young changemakers to influence policies, amplify children’s voices, and shape a better future. If you’re ready to lead, learn, and make an impact, don’t miss this opportunity. Apply now and be a voice for children across Europe. Discover more similar opportunities here. The post Join the Eurochild Children’s Council 2026–2028 | Fully Funded Youth Advocacy Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Agriculture in Trade Agreements: Fully Funded Online Course by UNITAR & FAO (Arabic, 2026)
summary: Understanding global agricultural trade is no longer optional—it is essential for shaping food security, economic growth, and sustainable development. The “Agriculture in Trade Agreements” online course, delivered through the United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) in collaboration with the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), provides a comprehensive learning experience designed to equip professionals with practical knowledge and policy-relevant skills in agricultural trade. This fully funded, four-week e-learning program offers participants a deep dive into international trade frameworks, regional agreements, and policy tools shaping the global agricultural landscape today. Key Details at a Glance Course Title: Agriculture in Trade Agreements Mode of Delivery: E-learning (Web-based) Language: Arabic Duration: 4 Weeks Course Dates: 28 June 2026 – 30 July 2026 Deadline to Apply: 1 June 2026 Cost: Free of charge Registration Type: Application-based (selection required) Programme Area: Public Finance and Trade Course Objectives By the end of this course, participants will be able to: Explain international trade frameworks Understand global regulatory systems governing trade Analyze outcomes of recent WTO ministerial conferences Evaluate non-tariff measures Assess their role in shaping international agricultural trade Understand WTO agreements influencing national trade policies Analyze regional trade agreements Identify emerging trends in regional trade agreements (RTAs) Compare regional agreements with multilateral frameworks Examine policy provisions affecting agricultural trade Background and Importance Agricultural trade plays a critical role in: Strengthening food security Supporting economic development Generating income opportunities , especially in developing regions As global agricultural markets become more integrated, countries face increasing pressure to balance: National development priorities International trade commitments This course was developed by FAO and delivered via UNITAR’s e-learning platform to address these challenges. It aims to strengthen the capacity of policymakers and professionals to design and implement trade policies that promote sustainable agricultural development . Participants will gain: Practical insights into trade negotiations Analytical tools for policy development Real-world examples from international trade systems Course Content and Structure The course is structured into three comprehensive modules , each containing 2–3 lessons: Module 1: WTO Agreement on Agriculture (AoA) Agriculture under GATT and market access rules Domestic support and export competition Implementation of AoA and Doha Round negotiations Module 2: Non-Tariff Measures in Agriculture Sanitary and phytosanitary measures (SPS) WTO SPS Agreement Technical barriers to trade (TBT) and related agreements Module 3: Agriculture in Regional Trade Agreements (RTAs) Trends in regional trade agreements Tariffs, quotas, and technical barriers Export restrictions, export duties, and rules of origin Learning Methodology The course adopts a flexible, self-paced learning model delivered over four weeks and includes: Mandatory and optional readings Case study exercises Interactive discussion forums Assessment quizzes Live webinars with international experts Participants will benefit from expert guidance and exposure to real-world policy scenarios, ensuring practical understanding and application. Target Audience This course is specifically designed for: Government officials Policymakers Professionals in agriculture and trade sectors Particularly those working in: Ministries of Agriculture Ministries of Economy Related governmental institutions Focus is placed on participants from the Near East and North Africa (NENA) region. Benefits for Applicants Gain in-depth expertise in global agricultural trade Learn from UN-backed institutions (UNITAR FAO) Strengthen your policy analysis and negotiation skills Enhance your professional profile with an international certificate Participate in a flexible, high-quality online learning experience Additional Information The course is completely free Delivered exclusively in Arabic Limited seats available Participants are selected by FAO based on: Geographic representation Gender balance Important Requirements: Applicants must obtain approval from their direct supervisor Successful participants will receive a certificate of completion Click here for more information and to apply Discover more global course opportunities on OFY here The post Agriculture in Trade Agreements: Fully Funded Online Course by UNITAR FAO (Arabic, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: European Court of Auditors AI Traineeship 2026: Paid EU Opportunity in Luxembourg (€2,500/Month)
summary: If you’re looking to break into artificial intelligence, data platforms, or EU institutions , the European Court of Auditors (ECA) is now accepting applications for its AI Traineeship Programme 2026 in Luxembourg. This is a highly competitive, paid EU traineeship offering hands-on experience in AI systems, data infrastructure, and digital transformation —all within one of the European Union’s key institutions. About the European Court of Auditors (ECA) The European Court of Auditors is the external auditor of the European Union , established in 1975. Based in Luxembourg, it plays a critical role in: Ensuring EU funds are used effectively Promoting accountability and transparency Evaluating EU policies and programmes With around 1,000 staff from across EU member states, the ECA offers a diverse, inclusive, and flexible working environment , including opportunities for teleworking and professional development. AI Traineeship Overview Location: Luxembourg Institution: European Court of Auditors (ECA) Duration: 6 months (renewable up to 12 months) Number of Positions: 2 trainees Monthly Allowance: €2,500 Deadline: 31 May 2026 (midnight Luxembourg time) This traineeship is designed for recent graduates and doctoral candidates seeking their first professional experience in an EU institution. What You’ll Gain Hands-on experience with AI systems and data platforms Mentorship from experienced EU professionals Exposure to enterprise-level AI and data infrastructure Opportunity to contribute to real-world EU digital transformation projects Work in a multicultural, international environment Available Traineeship Profiles 1. AI Model Architecture Infrastructure Focus: Artificial Intelligence IT Infrastructure You will support the development of on-premises AI solutions , ensuring data security, confidentiality, and technological sovereignty . Key Responsibilities: Configure and test open-source AI models (LLMs, OCR, document classification) Work on document analysis and automation pipelines Support retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) workflows Assist with AI agents and automation tools Deploy systems using Docker, Kubernetes, and Linux environments Document experiments and technical findings Ideal Background: Computer Science, AI, Data Science, or related field Python programming skills Knowledge of NLP and machine learning concepts Experience with Linux and containerization tools 2. AI-Ready Data Platform Architecture Analytics Focus: Data Platforms, Analytics Information Systems You will contribute to building a modern, AI-ready data ecosystem that supports auditors and analysts. Key Responsibilities: Design self-service data solutions Support data automation workflows Contribute to data integration and architecture design Collaborate with technical and business teams Document systems, processes, and use cases Ideal Background: Information Systems, Data Science, Engineering, or related field Strong interest in data platforms and AI technologies Python skills and familiarity with Linux environments Basic knowledge of databases and data processing Eligibility Criteria To apply, you must: Be a national of an EU member state Have completed a university degree (minimum 3 years) or be a PhD candidate Have no prior traineeship or employment in EU institutions Demonstrate strong language skills: C1 level in one EU language B2 level in another EU language English (minimum C1) required for AI projects Required Skills Competencies Analytical and problem-solving mindset Strong communication and documentation skills Willingness to learn and experiment Ability to work in a confidential and structured environment Interest in AI ethics, data protection, and secure systems Application Process Applications for the European Court of Auditors AI Traineeship 2026 will be reviewed by a selection committee . Candidates who best meet the requirements will be shortlisted and invited for an interview conducted by the committee. How to Apply Deadline: 31 May 2026 (midnight Luxembourg time) Language of Application: English or French Submission Method: Email Application Email: ECA-AI-traineeship@eca.europa.eu Required Documents Applicants must submit the following: Cover letter Up-to-date CV (preferably in Europass format ) Please indicate in your CV the language level according to the following assessment model: https://europa.eu/europass/en/common-european-framework-reference Recommendation letter Recommendation letter Important: Applications will be evaluated solely based on the documents submitted. Ensure all information is accurate and complete, as you may be asked to provide supporting evidence at a later stage. Applications must be submitted in English or French . Why You Should Apply This is more than just a traineeship—it’s a gateway into EU careers, AI innovation, and global policy impact . You’ll gain: Direct experience within an EU institution Exposure to cutting-edge AI and data systems A strong foundation for careers in international development, policy, or tech Equal Opportunities Inclusion The European Court of Auditors is committed to: Diversity and inclusion Equal opportunities regardless of background Supporting applicants with disabilities through reasonable accommodations FIND OUT MORE AND APPLY https://www.eca.europa.eu/en/traineeships https://www.eca.europa.eu/ContentPagesDocuments/Traineeships/ECA_Traineeship_in_AI_Call_EN.pdf Pro Tip: Tailor your application to highlight practical AI/data experience , even if from academic or personal projects. Demonstrating hands-on skills with Python, machine learning, or data systems will significantly strengthen your application. 🌐 For more INTERNSHIPS AND TRAINEESHIPS opportunities worldwide , visit: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=intern 🌐 For more AI-related opportunities worldwide: 👉 https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=artificial+intelligence 🇪🇺 For more opportunities for Europeans (internships, traineeships, jobs fellowships): 👉 https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=europe The post European Court of Auditors AI Traineeship 2026: Paid EU Opportunity in Luxembourg (€2,500/Month) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Education Changemakers Program 2026 (Cohort 3): Fully Funded Leadership Training, Mentorship & Seed Grants for Young African Leaders
summary: The Education Changemakers Program 2026 (Cohort 3) , led by the Regional Leadership Center East Africa in partnership with the Trevor Noah Foundation, is a highly competitive, fully funded leadership initiative designed to empower young African leaders working in the education sector. This transformative program offers a unique opportunity for educators, innovators, policymakers, and advocates across Africa to strengthen their leadership skills, expand their networks, and scale their impact in the education space. The initiative reflects a growing commitment to advancing educational transformation across the continent by investing in youth-led solutions and leadership development. Overview of the Education Changemakers Program 2026 The Education Changemakers Program is a 4-week intensive leadership experience that brings together young African professionals working in various areas of education to collaborate, learn, and innovate. Participants will engage in a highly interactive learning environment focused on: Leadership development Systems thinking in education Social innovation Policy and advocacy Impact scaling This program is designed not only to build skills but also to provide participants with the tools and support needed to create sustainable change within their communities and beyond. Program Timeline and Key Dates Applicants should carefully note the following important dates: Application Window: April 28 – May 13, 2026 Training Period: July 20 – August 14, 2026 Participants are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute challenges. Key Benefits of the Program The Education Changemakers Program offers a wide range of benefits that extend beyond traditional training programs. 1. Intensive Leadership Training Participants will receive structured, high-quality training focused on leadership in education, helping them develop critical thinking, decision-making, and problem-solving skills. 2. Coaching and Mentorship Each participant gains access to experienced mentors and coaches who provide guidance, support, and personalized feedback throughout the program. 3. Seed Grants for Impact Selected participants will have the opportunity to access seed funding to scale their educational initiatives and projects. 4. Pan-African Alumni Network Graduates become part of a vibrant and influential alumni network across Africa, opening doors to collaboration, partnerships, and future opportunities. 5. Fully Funded Participation There are no application or participation fees , making this program accessible to young leaders regardless of financial background. Who Should Apply? The program is open to young African leaders aged 18–35 who are actively engaged in the education sector. Eligible applicants include: Teachers and educators School leaders (e.g., head teachers) Social entrepreneurs EdTech innovators NGO leaders Researchers Policymakers Education advocates Applicants should demonstrate a clear commitment to improving education outcomes and driving positive change. Priority Consideration The program strongly encourages applications from individuals in: Rural communities Refugee or displaced backgrounds Minority or underserved communities This inclusive approach ensures that diverse perspectives are represented in shaping Africa’s education future. Program Structure and Learning Experience The 4-week program is designed to be immersive and practical. Participants will: Engage in interactive workshops and training sessions Collaborate with peers across different countries Work on real-world education challenges Develop scalable solutions and strategies Receive feedback from experts and mentors This structure ensures that participants leave the program with actionable insights and practical tools. Why This Program Matters Education remains one of the most powerful tools for development in Africa. However, the sector faces numerous challenges, including: Inequality in access Resource limitations Policy gaps Technological barriers The Education Changemakers Program addresses these challenges by equipping young leaders with the skills and resources needed to create innovative, scalable solutions. Application Process To apply, candidates must: Complete the official online application form Provide accurate personal and professional information Demonstrate leadership experience and impact in education Submit the application before the deadline Application Link: https://lnkd.in/dCaV2iMQ Contact Information For further inquiries and clarifications: Email: admissions@rlceastafrica.org Conclusion The Education Changemakers Program 2026 represents a powerful opportunity for young African leaders to enhance their leadership capacity, expand their networks, and access funding to scale their impact. By combining leadership training, mentorship, and financial support, the program serves as a catalyst for innovation and transformation in Africa’s education sector. Applicants are encouraged to apply early and present strong applications that reflect their passion, experience, and commitment to education. Discover more Global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE. The post Education Changemakers Program 2026 (Cohort 3): Fully Funded Leadership Training, Mentorship Seed Grants for Young African Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Embassy of Switzerland in Nepal National Programme Officer TVET Job 2026: Apply Before 3 May
summary: The Embassy of Switzerland in Nepal is inviting applications from qualified Nepali nationals for the position of National Programme Officer – Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) on a 2-year fixed-term contract , with the possibility of extension. This is an exciting opportunity for professionals in education, development, and vocational training to contribute to Nepal’s socio-economic growth while working with one of the country’s longstanding international development partners. Switzerland has been engaged in Nepal since 1959 through its development cooperation efforts, focusing on federal state building, employment and income generation, and migration . Since establishing its Embassy in Nepal in 2009, Switzerland has continued supporting Nepal’s development at national, provincial, and local levels. The National Programme Officer (NPO) will play a key role in strengthening Nepal’s TVET sector while supporting migration and vocational skills development initiatives. Position Summary Job Title: National Programme Officer – Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Organization: Embassy of Switzerland in Nepal Employment Type: Full-time (100%) Contract Duration: 2 years fixed-term contract (with possibility of extension) Personnel Category: Local Personnel Reporting To: Programme Manager – TVET, Migration and Climate Location: Nepal Application Deadline: 3 May 2026 at 5:00 PM NST About the Role The National Programme Officer will be responsible for the development, monitoring, and strategic steering of projects in the TVET sector, including vocational skills development within the migration portfolio. Under the supervision of the Programme Manager, the officer will ensure effective project cycle management, maintain high-quality implementation and reporting standards, and lead Switzerland’s policy engagement and donor coordination in Nepal’s TVET sector. The role also includes representing Switzerland in communications with government agencies, development partners, and private sector stakeholders. Key Responsibilities 1. Project Management and Programme Cycle Management (70%) The selected candidate will: Oversee planning, monitoring, implementation, and reporting of TVET projects Ensure project quality under the supervision of the Programme Manager Authorize payments according to approved annual plans and agreements Develop concept notes and credit proposals Participate in tender evaluation processes Support cross-border labour mobility pilots and labour market alignment initiatives Promote integration of transversal themes such as: Federal state building Digitalization Climate change Gender Equality and Social Inclusion (GESI) Contribute to annual planning, quarterly reviews, annual reports, and strategy development Support knowledge sharing and institutional learning 2. Policy Dialogue and Government Engagement (20%) Responsibilities include: Leading Switzerland’s policy and political engagement in Nepal’s TVET sector Supporting implementation of constitutional reforms related to TVET Strengthening private sector participation in TVET governance Enhancing quality improvement in vocational education systems Liaising with government officials at federal, provincial, and local levels Leading dialogue on migrant worker skilling and labour market alignment 3. Coordination, Networking, and Knowledge Management (5%) The officer will: Organize TVET donor group meetings Maintain donor coordination frameworks Ensure donor-supported projects align with TVET donor position papers Represent SDC in workshops, seminars, and policy forums Develop stories, case studies, and communication materials for websites and social media Strengthen collaboration across priority areas and partner institutions 4. Additional Responsibilities (5%) This includes: Supporting the design of cooperation strategies Contributing innovative ideas to improve Swiss development programming in Nepal Participating in the SDC Employment and Education Network Performing additional tasks as required for effective programme delivery Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following qualifications: Academic Qualifications A Master’s Degree in one of the following areas: TVET Education Economics Business Administration Or any other relevant field Professional Experience Applicants must have: At least 5 years of practical work experience in the development sector Minimum 3 years of project cycle management experience Experience in areas such as: TVET Private sector development Governance Migration-related programming Other relevant development sectors Required Competencies Technical Expertise Candidates should demonstrate: Strong understanding of labour markets and enterprise development Familiarity with Nepal’s TVET institutions including CTEVT and training providers Knowledge of federal, provincial, and local government systems Understanding of Nepal’s political and development landscape Experience working with government agencies, donors, and private sector actors Sensitivity to gender, equity, and social inclusion issues Proficiency in written and spoken English and Nepali Methodological Skills Applicants should be: Goal and results oriented Strong in analysis and conceptual thinking Capable of independent decision-making Innovative and adaptable Skilled in programme monitoring and management Social and Interpersonal Skills Candidates must possess: Excellent communication and networking skills Negotiation and teamwork abilities Constructive feedback handling Intercultural sensitivity Flexibility to handle diverse responsibilities How to Apply Applicants must submit the following documents: Application/Motivation Letter in English Updated CV/Bio-data Copy of Citizenship Certificate Academic Credentials References from former employer(s) All documents must be combined into one PDF attachment and sent via email. Email Address: kathmandu.application@eda.admin.ch Email Subject Line: National Programme Officer TVET Please do not submit Google Drive links or any other cloud storage links. Only PDF attachments will be processed. Diversity and Inclusion The Embassy of Switzerland in Nepal promotes workforce diversity and encourages applications from underrepresented and discriminated groups, including: Women Dalit communities Janajati groups Madhesi/Terai communities Other minority groups Positive discrimination will be applied in line with the Embassy’s diversity policy. Important Notes Only complete applications will be processed The Embassy will not respond to LinkedIn comments regarding this vacancy Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted via email The Embassy reserves the right to reject any or all applications without providing reasons This role presents an excellent opportunity for experienced professionals passionate about vocational education, labour market development, and policy reform in Nepal. If you have strong expertise in TVET, project management, and development cooperation, this could be your chance to contribute to national transformation while working with a globally respected development partner. Make sure your application reaches the Embassy before 3 May 2026 at 5:00 PM NST . Discover similar opportunities Here The post Embassy of Switzerland in Nepal National Programme Officer TVET Job 2026: Apply Before 3 May appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Pan-Atlantic University 2026: Fully Funded Scholarship for African Students in Nigeria
summary: The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Pan-Atlantic University (PAU) is a transformative, fully funded scholarship initiative designed to provide access to world-class education, leadership development, and career opportunities for exceptional young Africans facing financial and socio-economic barriers. As part of a long-term partnership with the Mastercard Foundation, Pan-Atlantic University is committed to nurturing ethical, service-driven leaders who will drive meaningful change across Africa. This initiative reflects a strong commitment to inclusion, equity, and sustainable development by prioritizing underserved and underrepresented groups. Overview of the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Pan-Atlantic University The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at PAU is a prestigious initiative aimed at empowering talented African youth through education and leadership training. The program focuses on individuals who demonstrate: Strong academic potential Leadership capacity A commitment to community impact Financial need Pan-Atlantic University is the first Nigerian university to partner with the Mastercard Foundation , with a goal of supporting 650 scholars over a ten-year period . Current Impact and Cohort Statistics The program has already made significant strides: 60 Scholars enrolled in the first cohort 80% are young women 20% are young men 6.7% are persons with disabilities 3.3% are refugees or displaced youths With the next intake, the university aims to admit an additional 70 scholars , further expanding its impact. What Makes This Scholarship Unique? This program is not just a scholarship—it is a holistic development pathway that combines academic excellence with leadership training and career readiness. It is specifically designed to remove barriers that prevent talented African youth from accessing higher education. Key Benefits of the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program Selected scholars will receive comprehensive support throughout their academic journey, including: 1. Full Financial Support Full tuition coverage Accommodation Living expenses 2. Leadership and Personal Development Structured mentorship programs Leadership training and capacity building Exposure to ethical leadership practices 3. Career and Professional Opportunities Internship placements Career guidance and support Access to professional networks 4. Networking and Global Exposure Engagement with a network of Mastercard Foundation Scholars Opportunities to connect with global leaders and changemakers Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following academic and personal requirements: Academic Requirements Must demonstrate academic excellence Must have passed WAEC, NECO, GCE, or NABTEB with: At least 5 credits , including: English Language Mathematics Three other relevant subjects Results must be obtained in not more than two sittings UTME Requirements Minimum UTME score of 220 Required subjects: English Language Any three subjects from Arts, Sciences, or Social Sciences Personal Requirements Demonstrated leadership potential Proven commitment to community impact Demonstrated financial need Priority Groups While the program is open to all qualified applicants, priority is given to: Young women, especially those interested in STEM fields Internally displaced persons and refugees Young people living with disabilities However, applicants outside these categories are still encouraged to apply if they meet the eligibility criteria. Program Goals and Vision The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at PAU aims to: Promote Inclusive Education Ensure access for marginalized and underserved groups Develop Ethical Leaders Equip scholars with values-driven leadership skills Foster Community Impact Encourage scholars to contribute to societal development Strengthen Africa’s Future Workforce Prepare graduates for impactful careers across sectors Selection Process The selection process is highly competitive and designed to identify candidates who demonstrate: Academic excellence Leadership ability Passion for social impact Financial need Applicants are encouraged to present strong applications that reflect both their academic achievements and their commitment to community development. Why You Should Apply The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Pan-Atlantic University offers a rare opportunity to: Access fully funded higher education in Nigeria Gain leadership and professional development skills Join a powerful network of African changemakers Build a career focused on impact and transformation This program is ideal for young Africans who are passionate about making a difference and are ready to take the next step in their academic and leadership journey. Application Deadline The deadline for application is 22nd May, 2026. Interested persons should CLICK HERE to submit their applications. Contact Information For further inquiries or application support, applicants can reach out via: Email: mastercardfoundationscholar@pau.edu.ng Phone: Ndidi: +234 708 864 1465 David: +234 701 782 5427 Lekan: +234 813 555 1765 General University Contacts: admissions@pau.edu.ng info@pau.edu.ng Office Hours: Monday to Friday 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM Location: Pan-Atlantic University Km 52, Lekki-Epe Expressway Ibeju-Lekki, Lagos, Nigeria To learn more about the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Pan-Atlantic University 2026, visit HERE. Discover more global Scholarship and African opportunities on OFY. title: PivotalResearch Fellowship 2026 in London: Fully Funded AI Safety Fellowship with £8,000 Stipend (Apply by May 3)
summary: Applications are now open for the Pivotal Research Fellowship 2026 Q3 , an exciting fully funded opportunity for individuals passionate about ensuring the safe development of artificial intelligence. Hosted at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA) , this prestigious fellowship offers participants the chance to spend 9 weeks in London , conducting high-impact AI safety research with expert mentorship, financial support, and the possibility of up to 6-month extensions . This fellowship is designed for aspiring and early-career researchers, policy thinkers, and professionals who want to contribute meaningfully to the future of AI governance, safety, evaluations, and control. About the Pivotal Research Fellowship Artificial intelligence is rapidly becoming one of the most transformative technologies of our time. The Pivotal Research Fellowship exists to support talented individuals committed to ensuring AI develops safely and responsibly. The program provides fellows with access to leading researchers, structured research management, a collaborative in-person workspace, and financial support to help them focus fully on impactful research. Since its launch, the fellowship has successfully completed 7 cohorts , supported 129 alumni , and maintains an impressive 9.1/10 peer recommendation score . Notably, 70–90% of fellows who apply for extensions receive continued support. Fellowship Location and Dates The 2026 Q3 fellowship will take place: Dates: June 29 – August 28, 2026 Location: London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA), London, United Kingdom Selected fellows will work in-person at LISA’s collaborative workspace, with weekday lunch and dinner provided. Financial Benefits The fellowship is fully funded and offers substantial support, including: £6,000 – £8,000 stipend Travel expenses to London covered £2,000 housing support for fellows coming from outside London Meals included during weekdays Access to required research compute resources Possibility of up to 6-month paid extensions This financial package ensures fellows can fully dedicate themselves to meaningful AI safety research without financial stress. What Fellows Receive Expert Mentorship Participants receive weekly one-on-one mentorship sessions with established researchers who provide: Methodological guidance Critical feedback Strategic career direction Research supervision Dedicated Research Management Pivotal’s research managers help fellows: Scope and refine projects Stay on track with deadlines Navigate research challenges Plan long-term career growth Workshops and Networking Fellows also benefit from: Q A sessions with leading AI safety experts Hands-on workshops Exposure to policymakers and researchers Expanded professional networks Eligibility Criteria The fellowship is open to: Anyone committed to ensuring AI develops safely There are no strict degree or nationality restrictions mentioned. Applicants from diverse academic and professional backgrounds are encouraged to apply, especially those interested in: AI safety AI governance AI policy Evaluations AI agents Corporate governance AI control AI security AI for biology (AIxBio) Applicants should demonstrate strong motivation and intellectual curiosity in the field. Mentors You Could Work With Participants are matched with leading researchers and experts, including: Jide Alaga — Research Scientist, Google DeepMind Cozmin Ududec — Head of Science of Evaluation, UK AISI Samira Nedungadi Jasper Götting — SecureBio Adam Kaufman — Redwood Research These mentors provide direct guidance throughout the fellowship. Why You Should Apply If you want to build a serious career in AI safety, policy, governance, or technical alignment research, this fellowship offers one of the strongest early-career opportunities available globally. With expert mentorship, full financial support, elite networking opportunities, and pathways into top institutions, the Pivotal Research Fellowship can significantly accelerate your impact and career trajectory. Application Process The selection process includes four stages: Step 1: Written Application Applicants submit: Personal background Research interests Mentor preferences Estimated time: 1–2 hours Step 2: Video Interview A short recorded interview. Estimated time: Less than 10 minutes Step 3: Work Task A mentor-specific research exercise. Estimated time: 1–3 hours Step 4: Personal Interview Interview with the Pivotal team or mentors. Estimated time: 30–45 minutes Application Timeline Important Dates Application Deadline: May 3, 2026 Decisions Released: May 22, 2026 Fellowship Period: June 29 – August 28, 2026 Extension Period: Up to 6 additional months Career Outcomes for Fellows Past fellows have gone on to work with top institutions including: Google DeepMind Anthropic Fellowship UK AISI GovAI SaferAI AI Futures Project Institute for Progress Cooperative AI Foundation FLI Beneficial AI Foundation Others have: Founded projects such as PRISM Evals, Catalyze Impact, and Moirai Started PhDs at Oxford, Stanford, EPFL, and Max Planck Institute Published research in top academic journals and policy forums Referral Bonus Opportunity Know someone who would be a strong fit? You can recommend a candidate and receive $1,000 if they are accepted into the fellowship. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their application before the deadline. Apply Here Apply for 2026 Q3 Fellowship You can also sign up to receive notifications for future fellowship cohorts. The future of AI will be shaped by the people working on it today. The Pivotal Research Fellowship 2026 Q3 offers a rare opportunity to join that effort, work alongside world-class mentors, and contribute to one of the most important challenges of our generation. If you are serious about building a career in AI safety and governance, this is an opportunity you should not miss. Deadline: May 3, 2026 Discover similar fellowship opportunities Here. title: BRAC BRIDGE Internship Programme 2026: Paid Global Development Internship for Students and Graduates
summary: Are you looking for a meaningful internship opportunity in international development? The BRAC BRIDGE Internship Programme 2026 offers a structured, paid pathway for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience with one of the world’s largest development organisations. This six-month programme is designed to connect academic learning with real-world development practice while preparing the next generation of global leaders. About BRAC BRAC is an international development organisation founded in Bangladesh in 1972. It works with over 100 million people worldwide to address inequality and poverty, focusing on areas such as education, health, livelihoods, climate change, and social innovation. The BRIDGE Internship Programme is part of BRAC’s long-term commitment to developing young professionals who can contribute to solving global development challenges. BRAC BRIDGE Internship Programme Overview The BRIDGE Internship Programme is a six-month, paid internship targeted at final-year students and recent graduates. It provides an opportunity to work on real development projects while gaining exposure to both field and office environments. During the programme, interns will: Work on projects across different regions in Bangladesh Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams Engage with real-world social and economic challenges Learn from experienced professionals and mentors Contribute to impactful development initiatives Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Bachelor’s degree from a recognised university or currently in the final semester Completion of all coursework (for students awaiting internship) Open to all academic disciplines Demonstrated interest in development work Participation in extracurricular activities is an added advantage Key Responsibilities Selected interns will: Support day-to-day operations of assigned projects Collaborate with departments, programmes, and field offices Prepare reports, presentations, and research outputs Contribute ideas to address development challenges Assist in organising meetings, events, and field activities Develop analytical and research-oriented problem-solving skills Internship Benefits Participants in the BRAC internship programme will receive: Monthly stipend of BDT 8,000 Certificate and Letter of Appreciation upon completion Mentorship from experienced professionals Exposure to large-scale development programmes Opportunities to work in both office and field settings Access to BRAC University library and research resources Subsidised meals and transportation support (conditions apply) Potential pathway to full-time employment at BRAC Why Apply for a BRAC Internship Key reasons to consider this opportunity include: Work with one of the world’s largest development organisations Gain practical, hands-on experience in international development Build professional skills relevant to global careers Receive mentorship and career guidance Increase chances of securing long-term roles within BRAC Internship Areas and Departments Interns may be placed across a wide range of programmes, including: Advocacy for Social Change BRAC International Central Compliance Climate Change Communications Education Global Partnerships and Thought Leadership Health Human Resource Division Learning and Leadership Development Microfinance Migration Otithi Technology Social Innovation Lab Youth Platform Types of BRAC Internship Programmes BRIDGE Internship Programme (6 Months) Designed for final-year students and recent graduates Focus on in-depth learning and professional development Includes mentorship and potential transition to full-time roles Regular Internship Programme (3 Months) Open to undergraduate and postgraduate students Provides short-term exposure to BRAC’s programmes Focus on skill development and practical experience Application Process To apply for a BRAC internship: Review available internship opportunities Complete the official application form Prepare and submit: Updated CV (including passport-size photo) Letter of intention Email application to: internship@brac.net Clearly indicate the programme or department in the subject line Applications are assessed based on academic qualifications and relevant experience. Successful candidates are typically notified within two weeks after the application deadline. Application Deadline 10 May 2026 Apply Now BRIDGE Internship Programme Application Form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdJxm9FpxH-Oso4rsnzq9OA76APRmZ5RKRk2PL_rc-M-DAY4Q/viewform https://www.brac.net/get-involved/internship/bridge-internship/ Internship Programme: https://www.brac.net/regular-internship Frequently Asked Questions Where are BRAC internships based? Internships are currently offered in Bangladesh. Are international applicants eligible? Yes. BRAC accepts international applicants, and English is widely used as a working language. Is the internship paid? Yes. Interns receive a monthly stipend. Is knowledge of Bangla required? No. Translator support is available if needed. Is accommodation provided? Accommodation is not provided, but guidance is available for international interns. What is expected of interns? Interns are expected to contribute to research, reporting, fieldwork, and presentations within their assigned departments. Conclusion The BRAC BRIDGE Internship Programme 2026 provides a strong foundation for anyone seeking a career in international development. With structured learning, mentorship, and real-world exposure, it offers a valuable opportunity to build skills while contributing to meaningful global impact. Applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare strong application materials that demonstrate their interest in development work and readiness to contribute. title: Just Tech Fellowship 2027 by Social Science Research Council (SSRC): $60,000 Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers, Creatives, and Practitioners in Technology & Society
summary: The Just Tech Fellowship 2027 , offered by the Social Science Research Council (SSRC), is a prestigious and fully funded opportunity designed for individuals working at the intersection of technology and society . With an unrestricted award of up to $60,000 , this fellowship empowers researchers, artists, technologists, and practitioners to explore how technology shapes public life and to develop innovative, impactful solutions. This one-year fellowship program is not just about funding—it is a transformative platform that supports interdisciplinary collaboration, public engagement, and forward-thinking research that addresses some of the most pressing societal challenges driven by technological change. What is the Just Tech Fellowship? The Just Tech Fellowship is a flagship initiative of the Social Science Research Council aimed at supporting community-grounded, original, and socially impactful work that critically examines the role of technology in shaping society. The fellowship welcomes applicants from diverse backgrounds, including: Social scientists Humanists Artists Journalists Technologists Community-based researchers Public-interest practitioners The program emphasizes interdisciplinary collaboration , ensuring that fellows contribute to a broader understanding of how digital systems influence governance, equity, public discourse, and everyday life. What the Fellowship Offers Selected fellows receive a comprehensive package of financial and professional support: Financial Award Up to $60,000 unrestricted funding to support: Research projects Creative work Community-based initiatives Professional Development Monthly virtual cohort gatherings Individualized mentorship support Access to the Just Tech network Collaboration Opportunities One in-person workshop for fellows Opportunities to collaborate across cohorts Eligibility for additional seed funding for joint projects Long-Term Engagement Continued access to the Just Tech network beyond the fellowship year Fellowship Duration Fellowship Period: January 2027 – December 2027 The program spans one full year, allowing fellows to deeply engage with their projects while participating in structured activities and collaborative exchanges. Fellowship Objectives The Just Tech Fellowship aims to: Advance Critical Research Support projects that interrogate how technology affects society Promote Public Engagement Encourage fellows to share their work with broader audiences Foster Interdisciplinary Dialogue Build bridges across fields and practices Support Innovative Thinking Enable creative and experimental approaches to technology-related challenges Areas of Focus Applicants are encouraged to propose projects that engage with: Digital technologies and public life Technology governance and accountability Artificial intelligence and ethics Data justice and privacy Social media and public discourse Community-centered tech innovation Technology and inequality Projects may involve: Research Creative production Policy engagement Community collaboration Application Requirements Applicants must submit a comprehensive application including: 1. Résumé/CV Maximum of 2 pages 2. Personal Statement Up to 1,000 words OR Video statement (maximum 5 minutes) 3. Work Proposal Written document (max 3,000 words) OR Project deck (up to 10 slides) The proposal must include: Concept description Engagement with technology Approach and contribution Feasibility plan Field context Public contribution 4. Work Samples Two samples showcasing previous work Brief descriptions explaining your role 5. AI Use Disclosure Applicants may use AI tools for assistance, but submissions must reflect original ideas and voice Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Residency Open to citizens of any country Must reside in the United States during the fellowship period SSRC does not sponsor visas Education No formal degree requirements Applicants may come from academic or non-academic backgrounds Availability Must commit to the full fellowship year Restrictions Full-time students are not eligible Experience Demonstrated engagement with issues at the intersection of technology and society Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on: Articulated Practice Clear and coherent body of work Strength of Project Relevance and originality Execution Capacity Ability to complete the project Public Engagement Evidence of sharing work with audiences Program Fit Alignment with fellowship goals Collaboration Willingness to engage with peers and contribute to the cohort Application Timeline Application Opens: April 27, 2026 Application Deadline: June 28, 2026 (11:59 p.m. EST) Fellows Notified: November 2026 Fellowship Period: January – December 2027 Why You Should Apply The Just Tech Fellowship stands out because it: Provides substantial funding ($60,000) Supports interdisciplinary innovation Encourages real-world impact and public engagement Connects fellows to a global network of experts Offers flexibility and creative freedom Click HERE to learn more and to apply Contact Information For inquiries, applicants can contact: just-tech@ssrc.org Discover more global Fellowship and Tech opportunities on OFY.
